definition: Falling headline inflation loosens financial conditions while the underlying inflation regime is unresolved, extending credit cycles by 12-18 months. Documented across 4/5 historical episodes (1967-68, 1984-85, 1995-96, 2005-06). The 1995-96 exception required productivity growth at 2.5%+ to validate the loosening ex post.
evidence: Causal mechanism: falling headline → loosened conditions → extended credit cycle → delayed but amplified correction
1995-96 exception required productivity growth to validate loosening ex post
Directly challenges near-term credit repricing timing assumptions
open_questions: Is current productivity growth sufficient to validate loosening (2.5%+ threshold)?
How does the paradox interact with covenant-lite and fiscal support in the current cycle?
What is the magnitude of the eventual correction after the extended cycle?
